title: Silent Steps in the Study
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned art curator, was found dead in the cluttered study. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Harlow
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the water bottle and did not go near the shoe.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Fiona McAllister
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Trevor Sinclair helped me move the remote control aside. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Gareth Harlow and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Trevor Sinclair and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Trevor Sinclair
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, I helped Fiona McAllister move the remote control aside. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Fiona McAllister and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

clues.title: Granules in Water Bottle
clues.detail: Undissolved blue granules cling below the liquid line inside the water bottle. The same blue residue is visible at Eleanor Whitcombe's lips and in the remaining drink.

clues.title: Position of Remote Control
clues.detail: The fresh contact mark left by the remote control is crossed by a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink whose edge remains unbroken. Fiona McAllister and Trevor Sinclair both state that they moved the remote control aside at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Water Bottle
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught around the handle and rim of the water bottle. Gareth Harlow's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Fragment at Shoe
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged in a narrow edge of the shoe on top of a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Gareth Harlow has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Mark on Backpack
clues.detail: A dark mark crosses the backpack. The same intact factory finish continues over the marked area.

solution.motive: Gareth Harlow killed Eleanor Whitcombe because the discovery that Eleanor had forged his signature on a multimillion‑pound art sale, jeopardising his financial future.
solution.method: A fatal crushed dose was dissolved in the water bottle before Eleanor Whitcombe drank.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Gareth Harlow claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Fiona McAllister and Trevor Sinclair left together at 8:35 pm; the fine copper-coloured silk threads on the water bottle and the black and gold fragment recovered from the shoe place Gareth Harlow inside afterwards. Gareth Harlow therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Eleanor Whitcombe and reach the water bottle during the murder window.
